The Robotech kickstarter and RIFTS (RPG) kickstarter drove a friend of mine to give up on Palladium Publishing entirely. I mean, I'm no Palladium fan at all. But you guys know how I am about AD&D? This buddy of mine was like that about Palladium. Yes, for real. And he just dropped it all after the debacle with the RIFTS Savage Worlds KS and Robotech RPG Tactics Wave 2 nonsense.

Don't blame him.

This buddy of mine was like that about Palladium. Yes, for real. And he just dropped it all after the debacle with the RIFTS Savage Worlds KS and Robotech RPG Tactics Wave 2 nonsense.

Don't blame him.
Click to expand...

The Robotech Tactics KS is a well-documented, ongoing clusterfuck, but what about Savage Rifts ticked him off?

Legendary Pubber

377

  • May 11, 2017

The Butcher said:
The Robotech Tactics KS is a well-documented, ongoing clusterfuck, but what about Savage Rifts ticked him off?
Click to expand...

Just fallout; disgusted with KS to the point that he can't separate the game from the person.
 

The Butcher

Legendary Pubber
Joined
Apr 29, 2017
Messages
4,840
Reaction score
12,985
  • May 11, 2017
  • #15
thedungeondelver said:
Just fallout; disgusted with KS to the point that he can't separate the game from the person.
Click to expand...

His disgust is misguided, as Siembieda had nothing to do with Savage Rifts beyond giving Sean Patrick Fannon permission to the (once unthinkable) conversion.

Palladium has been missing their own deadlines since the 1990s. There is absolutely no reason to believe they'd deliver crowdfunded stuff in a timely manner.

Not to mention the abundant anecdotes suggesting that Siembieda, whatever his merits as a creator and as someone who's kept a RPG publisher afloat for 30+ years, is something of a douche.

Spinachcat

Legendary Pubber

Apr 27, 2017
Messages
762
Reaction score
1,154
  • May 14, 2017
  • #17
Tristram, what minis rules do you use the figs with? The Nightmare Legion artwork is classic Warhammer from the Oldhammer days. Is it the same artist or a retro-homage?

As for the Rifts Boardgame, it was cancelled. I feel bad for Carmen. I gamed with him and hung out at the Palladium Open House years ago and I found him to be a truly good dude. The whole Robotech forum incident and his overly publicized suicide attempt were terrible, but sadly he and his Rogue Heroes team launched their Rifts Boardgame KS extremely poorly. They hit $67k out of $100k needed and the comment section was an insane disaster.

Sadly, both KS are worth studying by anyone interested in launching their own. Just as CMON and Sine Nomine are worth studying to learn how to do the right thing, these two KS are worth studying to avoid the wrong things. Such a shame.
 

TristramEvans

The Right Hand of Doom
Administrator
Moderator
Joined
Apr 24, 2017
Messages
40,398
Reaction score
124,996
  • May 14, 2017
  • #18
Spinachcat said:
Tristram, what minis rules do you use the figs with?
Click to expand...

Warhammer 3rd mainly. I'm working on my own system, which is sort of an adaption of Hail Caesar to fantasy, which in turn was an adaption of Warmaster to historical/28mm.

The Nightmare Legion artwork is classic Warhammer from the Oldhammer days. Is it the same artist or a retro-homage?
Click to expand...

I was just saying the two would go well together, different artists/sculptors on the Kickstarter, using renaissance artwork as inspiration. In general, though, the sculpts have a "retro-vibe", in that they evoke 80s Citadel designs.

Spinachcat said:
As for the Rifts Boardgame, it was cancelled. I feel bad for Carmen. I gamed with him and hung out at the Palladium Open House years ago and I found him to be a truly good dude. The whole Robotech forum incident and his overly publicized suicide attempt were terrible, but sadly he and his Rogue Heroes team launched their Rifts Boardgame KS extremely poorly. They hit $67k out of $100k needed and the comment section was an insane disaster.

Sadly, both KS are worth studying by anyone interested in launching their own. Just as CMON and Sine Nomine are worth studying to learn how to do the right thing, these two KS are worth studying to avoid the wrong things. Such a shame.
Click to expand...

The RRT guys shitting up the comments may have scared people away, but I think it was two things they had nothing to do with. The fulfillment was two years off, and the stretch goals were shit. Asking 100k, and at 130k the rulebook is color? WTF? Miniature KS go into the stratosphere because they stretch goal more minis. You get to where you can't afford NOT to back the KS and pow, you bust a million.
 

TristramEvans

  • May 14, 2017
  • #22
after the way Carmen introduced himself to the Palladium KS audience, I cant feel sorry for him at all
 

CRKrueger

Eläytyminatör
Joined
Apr 25, 2017
Messages
14,645
Reaction score
36,483
  • May 14, 2017
  • #23
TristramEvans said:
after the way Carmen introduced himself to the Palladium KS audience, I cant feel sorry for him at all
Click to expand...
Forgetting all the drama, he made a critical mistake as a game designer.

Successful miniatures game Kickstarters are not about your game, as much as they are about your minis.
You need the rules done, you need more actual sculpts, you need a stretch goals structure that incentivizes people backing early and not waiting til the last minute and generates buzz. People need to know they are backing to actually fund the production of these materials, not backing to design the materials in the first place, ie. paying your rent.
 

Spinachcat

OK, this is something I've wanted in miniature form for a LONG time....



Napoleon as represented in the classic painting "Bonaparte Crossing the Alps" by Jacques-Louis David



Foundry miniatures did a limited edition one a long time ago, but as you can see, it doesn't quuuuuuiiiiiite capture the painting...
